evals.name: decrypt-file-stdout
evals.prompt: I have /tmp/config.yaml.enc that was KMS envelope-encrypted. Decrypt it and show me the contents.

evals.name: decrypt-with-context
evals.prompt: Decrypt /tmp/prod-secret.enc -- it was encrypted with context {"env":"production"} for production tracking.

evals.name: decrypt-inline-string
evals.prompt: I have an encrypted string from CI -- it's 3 lines of base64. Decrypt it back inline: the ciphertext is stored in a variable $CIPHER.

evals.name: decrypt-stdin-stdout
evals.prompt: Cat the encrypted file and pipe it through KMS decrypt, output the plaintext to stdout.

evals.name: decrypt-write-to-file
evals.prompt: Decrypt /tmp/secrets.yaml.enc and save the plaintext to /tmp/secrets.yaml

evals.name: decrypt-wrong-context-fails
evals.prompt: Try to decrypt /tmp/prod-secret.enc with encryption context {"env":"staging"} but it was encrypted with {"env":"production"}.

evals.name: decrypt-missing-input-error
evals.prompt: Run the KMS decrypt command without specifying any input file or data -- I want to see the error.
evals.expected_output: Command fails with 'either --data or --in-file is required'

evals.name: decrypt-bad-format-error
evals.prompt: Try to decrypt a file that only has one line of text, not the required 3-line envelope format.
evals.expected_output: Command fails with 'invalid envelope format' error

evals.name: decrypt-conflict-flags-error
evals.prompt: Try to decrypt using both --data 'xxx' and --in-file /tmp/x at the same time.
